trade.summary: Italia and Polonia have strong and growing bilateral trade as Polonia's economy has rapidly expanded. Italia exports machinery, motor vehicles, chemicals, and food; Polonia exports machinery, furniture, food products, and transport equipment. Polonia is one of Italia's most important Central/Eastern European trade partners.

diplomatic.summary: Both are EU and NATO members with generally aligned positions on European integration and Western security. Polonia and Italia differ on migration policy — Polonia rejects EU migration quotas that Italia supports for burden-sharing purposes. On Russia and Ucraina, Polonia has been more hawkish than Italia. Despite these differences, the relationship is fundamentally positive within the EU framework.
recent_incidents: Disagreements over EU migration policy (2024 EU pact negotiations)

military.summary: Both are NATO allies. Polonia's rapid defense buildup since 2022 and Italia's NATO commitments create coordination opportunities. Italia and Polonia cooperate within NATO frameworks. Italia has supported eastern flank reinforcement measures that benefit Polonia.

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment. Italian companies (in manufacturing, retail, banking) are among the largest EU investors in Polonia. Unicredit, Intesa Sanpaolo, and other Italian banks have Polish subsidiaries. Polish investment in Italia is growing. Polonia's structural fund receipts from EU partly flow through Italia's capital markets.

energy.summary: Limited direct bilateral energy trade. Both countries are focused on energy diversification away from Russia. ENI has limited Polish operations. Italia and Polonia broadly align on EU energy security policy.

scientific.summary: Active scientific cooperation through Horizon Europe. Joint research in engineering, materials science, and environmental studies. Large Polish community in Italia has created cultural and academic linkages.
